source_episode: Boris Cherny
source_quote_or_context: What I really missed about Ant was the mission... even if it's building a really cool product, it's just not really a substitute for that.
core_belief: Mission-fit beats product excitement for sustained builder energy.
operational_pattern: When evaluating job moves, weight mission alignment above product novelty; expect fast reversals when mission gap appears.
organizational_archetype: Senior IC/founder choosing between labs and product companies
hidden_assumptions: Great products compensate for weak mission
pressure_scenarios: Burnout on technically exciting but empty work
contradictions: Claims mission-first but optimizes for product hype
operational_adaptations: Write mission criteria before entertaining offers
Use short trials with explicit mission checkpoints
ai_era_implications: AI talent war makes mission the retention moat when models commoditize

source_episode: Boris Cherny
source_quote_or_context: You want to under-resource things a little bit at the start... if you have one engineer on a project, they're forced to Claude-ify.
core_belief: Constraint plus great engineers produces disproportionate agent leverage.
operational_pattern: Staff lean squads on new bets; pair unlimited tokens with small headcount; optimize cost only after idea proves out.
pressure_scenarios: Board pressure to cut AI spend early
contradictions: Preaches lean teams while company headcount grows 4x
behavioral_signals: Many engineers on one agentic feature
emotional_political_dynamics: Fear that AI makes headcount look wasteful
operational_adaptations: Single-owner projects with explicit speed KPIs
ai_era_implications: Default org design becomes small teams orchestrating many agents

pattern_name: Terminal Form Factor For Model Velocity
source_episode: Boris Cherny
source_quote_or_context: The model is improving so quickly, we felt there wasn't really another form factor that could keep up... the terminal was the only idea that I had.
core_belief: Fastest-improving models need the least prescriptive UI shell.
operational_pattern: Prefer minimal, composable surfaces (CLI/terminal) until model capabilities stabilize; add IDE layers after workflow clarity.
organizational_archetype: AI coding product team racing model releases
hidden_assumptions: Users need polished IDE on day one
Terminal is too alien for adoption
contradictions: Ships terminal while marketing polished apps
behavioral_signals: Power users in CLI; mass market in GUI wrappers
emotional_political_dynamics: Design vs infra tension on form factor
operational_adaptations: DAU charts from week one per Ben Mann advice
ai_era_implications: Agent UIs stay thin; value migrates to orchestration and integrations

source_episode: Boris Cherny
source_quote_or_context: If you make people feel heard, then they want to contribute... now Claude does a lot of the work on internal feedback channels.
core_belief: Tight feedback loops beat roadmap certainty in agentic products.
operational_pattern: Instrument internal feedback firehose; fix or agent-fix within minutes early on; point agents at Slack/telemetry for ideation.
organizational_archetype: Head of fast-moving devtool with vocal power users
hidden_assumptions: Roadmaps should be quarterly and fixed
contradictions: Celebrates user voice while prioritizing stealth features
behavioral_signals: PMs defensive about auto-PRs from agents
operational_adaptations: Public fix streaks to encourage more signal
ai_era_implications: PM work shifts from prioritization queues to taste and strategy

source_episode: Boris Cherny
source_quote_or_context: We really just think in exponentials... all I did was trace the line on percent of code written by Claude.
core_belief: Linear intuition systematically under-forecasts AI adoption curves.
operational_pattern: Force exponential scenarios in planning; personal dogfood to 100% AI code before assuming industry lag.
organizational_archetype: Lab leadership forecasting dev tool impact
failure_modes: Planning as if 20% AI code is ceiling
Under-investing in safety for agentic tools
contradictions: Public exponential talk; internal linear resourcing
behavioral_signals: Gasps at conferences then slow internal migration
operational_adaptations: Publish leading indicators not lagging surveys
ai_era_implications: Organizations that don't plan exponentially get surprised by capacity and security needs

source_episode: Boris Cherny
source_quote_or_context: To do good work you really have to understand the layer under the layer... with AI, you have to understand the model.
core_belief: Effective builders still descend one abstraction level.
operational_pattern: Onboard via month hacking plus post-training exposure; expect staff to debug via agents but understand runtime/model limits.
organizational_archetype: Engineering org mixing researchers and product builders
hidden_assumptions: Vibe coding removes need to understand systems
failure_modes: Blind trust in agent output on prod
contradictions: New grads out-agent veterans on novel bugs
ai_era_implications: Understanding models becomes core eng literacy like compilers once were

source_episode: Boris Cherny
source_quote_or_context: Literacy went from sub-1% scribes to 70% over 200 years after the printing press... programming may democratize similarly.
core_belief: Tool revolutions expand who can participate, not eliminate experts overnight.
operational_pattern: Frame AI coding as literacy expansion; invest in education/time-for-learning, not gatekeeping syntax.
organizational_archetype: Industry leader communicating AI societal impact
operational_adaptations: Pair historical analogs with safety work
Make building delightful to widen funnel
ai_era_implications: Builder population explodes; expert role shifts to orchestration and judgment

source_episode: Boris Cherny
source_quote_or_context: By the end of the year everyone is going to be a product manager, and everyone codes... software engineer title will be replaced by builder.
core_belief: Role boundaries dissolve when execution cost drops.
operational_pattern: Train hybrid builders; expect PM/eng/design overlap; staff for outcomes not function silos.
contradictions: Everyone claims builder; few ship end-to-end
operational_adaptations: Comp bands tied to impact not craft
ai_era_implications: Performance reviews based on shipped outcomes and agent leverage

source_episode: Sequoia CEO coach: Why it's never been easier to start a company, and never been harder to scale one | Brian Halligan (co-founder, HubSpot)
source_quote_or_context: Perpetual state of constructive dissatisfaction... always a little bit dissatisfied with where they are, very focused on the end state.
core_belief: Restless dissatisfaction paired with humility beats victory laps.
operational_pattern: Coach CEOs to stay future-anchored; celebrate wins briefly; channel dissatisfaction into exec-team quality not self-flagellation.
operational_adaptations: Kids vs adults CEO tables for stage-fit problems
Peer misery loops on hard feedback
ai_era_implications: AI multiplies execution options—dissatisfaction must focus which bets not to take

source_episode: Sequoia CEO coach: Why it's never been easier to start a company, and never been harder to scale one | Brian Halligan (co-founder, HubSpot)
source_quote_or_context: Almost every time we hired the three out of four... we went with the spikier people, people with weaknesses who would challenge stuff.
core_belief: Executive impact comes from spikes and challenge, not balanced mediocrity.
operational_pattern: Score interviews 1–4; prefer polarized panels over uniform 3/4; shrink panel size to four.
organizational_archetype: CEO hiring first C-suite at ~50–500 employees
contradictions: Claims to want challengers; punishes pushback
ai_era_implications: AI resumes hide spikes—interviews must test live challenge and taste

source_episode: Sequoia CEO coach: Why it's never been easier to start a company, and never been harder to scale one | Brian Halligan (co-founder, HubSpot)
source_quote_or_context: Would you enthusiastically rehire this person for that role? ... on a scale of one to ten, how likely you'll try to rehire them back.
core_belief: Reference quality is revealed by question difficulty not checkbox strengths.
operational_pattern: Probe references with rehire/top-percentile questions; detect box-checking vs genuine investigation.
emotional_political_dynamics: Founders hear only positives they want
ai_era_implications: AI background checks don't replace human rehire probes

source_episode: Sequoia CEO coach: Why it's never been easier to start a company, and never been harder to scale one | Brian Halligan (co-founder, HubSpot)
source_quote_or_context: Massive impedance mismatch when you hire Microsoft/Salesforce exec at 50 employees... 100% attrition on those folks.
core_belief: Prestige resumes misfit startup chaos tolerance.
operational_pattern: Avoid logo hires expecting mature processes; favor homegrown or true startup scar tissue.
organizational_archetype: Scaling startup tempted by FAANG VPs
behavioral_signals: Execs surprised by lack of ops
operational_adaptations: Red Sox model: homegrown core plus few free agents
ai_era_implications: AI lets small teams output big-co volume—less need for big-co managers

source_episode: Sequoia CEO coach: Why it's never been easier to start a company, and never been harder to scale one | Brian Halligan (co-founder, HubSpot)
source_quote_or_context: Massive tax in optionality when you can move this fast... planning cycles now three months.
core_belief: Speed punishes indecision and one-way door paralysis.
operational_pattern: Shorten planning horizons; walk through one-way doors faster; avoid second-act hops before beachhead depth.
organizational_archetype: CEO with AI-accelerated roadmap surface area
operational_adaptations: Peer tables to practice faster decisions
Explicit kill criteria for side bets
ai_era_implications: More projects possible—optionality tax rises without ruthless focus

source_episode: Sequoia CEO coach: Why it's never been easier to start a company, and never been harder to scale one | Brian Halligan (co-founder, HubSpot)
source_quote_or_context: If you want to kill a plant, have two people water it... adults' table zealots on DRI.
core_belief: Cross-functional outcomes require single accountable owner with authority.
operational_pattern: Name one DRI per pilot/process; give power across silos; avoid co-ownership on critical metrics.
ai_era_implications: Agent handoffs still need human DRI or brooms flood the castle

source_episode: Sequoia CEO coach: Why it's never been easier to start a company, and never been harder to scale one | Brian Halligan (co-founder, HubSpot)
source_quote_or_context: When you have to eat a shit sandwich, don't nibble... rip the Band-Aid off on layoffs and bad news.
core_belief: Partial painful actions compound trauma.
operational_pattern: Do single decisive corrections; pair with 'next play' culture after errors.
organizational_archetype: CEO facing valuation reset or outage
ai_era_implications: AI efficiency may force one clean reset not rolling trims

source_episode: Sequoia CEO coach: Why it's never been easier to start a company, and never been harder to scale one | Brian Halligan (co-founder, HubSpot)
source_quote_or_context: Lovable, Obsessed, Chip on shoulder, Knowledgeable... student of the game.
core_belief: Founder quality is multi-trait pattern match not charisma alone.
operational_pattern: Screen for followership, obsession depth, domain knowledge, learning velocity; add student mindset for scaling skills.
hidden_assumptions: Anyone can be CEO with effort
contradictions: LOCK talk for investability not coaching
operational_adaptations: Five-tool CEO rare but rising with AI
ai_era_implications: AI fills skill gaps but not lovable/trust/craft obsession

source_episode: Sherwin Wu V2
source_quote_or_context: IC engineers are becoming tech leads... managing fleets and fleets of agents.
core_belief: Engineering job is parallel agent orchestration not typing.
operational_pattern: Run 10–20 agent threads; steer/review; treat prompts as incantations with senior judgment.
contradictions: PR volume up 70% for power users
ai_era_implications: Sorcerer's apprentice risk scales with autonomous agents

source_episode: Sherwin Wu V2
source_quote_or_context: Team maintaining 100% Codex-written codebase without rolling up sleeves manually.
core_belief: Forcing agent-only path surfaces context and documentation gaps.
operational_pattern: Run no-manual-code pilots; encode tribal knowledge into skills/md/comments when agents fail.
ai_era_implications: Teams that never remove escape hatch won't learn agent-native workflows

source_episode: Sherwin Wu V2
source_quote_or_context: When the coding agent is not doing what you want, it's usually a problem with context.
core_belief: Underspecified context dominates model capability gaps.
operational_pattern: Add docs, structure, skills files; treat fixes as information architecture not model swaps.
ai_era_implications: Context engineering becomes primary senior eng skill

source_episode: Sherwin Wu V2
source_quote_or_context: We hand to models the things that annoy us... code review was one of the worst things for me.
core_belief: Delegate loathed engineering toil to agents first for adoption.
operational_pattern: 100% Codex PR review; automate lint/CI fixes; collapse deploy friction.
organizational_archetype: EM who hated reviews owning newsfeed at Quora
hidden_assumptions: AI must start with greenfield features
failure_modes: Circular self-review without human spot checks
ai_era_implications: Review and CI toil automated before greenfield coding

source_episode: Sherwin Wu V2
source_quote_or_context: Build for where the models are going, not where they are today... this is the worst the models will ever be.
core_belief: Scaffolding has short half-life; bet on capability curves.
operational_pattern: Minimize durable scaffolding; ship for next-quarter models; treat customer requests skeptically when field moves weekly.
organizational_archetype: Platform product leader in fast-moving model market
contradictions: Listening to customers is always right
behavioral_signals: Kevin Weil quote as planning anchor
emotional_political_dynamics: FOMO on shipping for today's customers
ai_era_implications: Product orgs that listen only to today's users under-shoot

source_episode: Sherwin Wu V2
source_quote_or_context: Spend more than 50% of your time with top 10% performers... manager as scalpel supplier in surgery room.
core_belief: Manager leverage is unblocking top performers not equal time spread.
operational_pattern: Majority time on top decile; pre-clear organizational blockers; anticipate needs.
organizational_archetype: EM at API platform powering AI economy
operational_adaptations: AI to scan org docs for blockers
ai_era_implications: Manager span increases when AI handles context gathering

source_episode: Sherwin Wu V2
source_quote_or_context: Codex empowers top performers... broader spread in team productivity.
core_belief: AI amplifies agency; distribution widens.
operational_pattern: Identify high-agency engineers; let them experiment publicly; share practices org-wide.
ai_era_implications: Comp and leveling must account for 10x individuals

source_episode: Sherwin Wu V2
source_quote_or_context: Enable one-person billion-dollar startup... hundred other small startups building bespoke software.
core_belief: Extreme leverage fragments tooling into vertical micro-SaaS.
operational_pattern: Expect startup boom, vertical AI tools, B2B golden age; VC returns may concentrate in platforms.
contradictions: Lenny's newsletter support objection vs bespoke vendors
ai_era_implications: Distribution and trust become scarcer than build capability

pattern_name: Hire Yourself Via Build In Public
source_episode: The rise of the professional vibe coder (a new AI-era job) | Lazar Jovanovic (Professional Vibe Coder)
source_quote_or_context: You don't need a company to hire you. You can hire yourself as a professional vibe coder first.
core_belief: Demonstrated public shipping beats waiting for role creation.
operational_pattern: Publish builds; create demand; let companies retroactively define the job around you.
organizational_archetype: Non-technical builder aspiring to full-time vibe role
ai_era_implications: Professional vibe coder becomes formal headcount category

source_episode: The rise of the professional vibe coder (a new AI-era job) | Lazar Jovanovic (Professional Vibe Coder)
source_quote_or_context: Positively delusional that everything is possible until proven wrong... but self-aware you must get better.
core_belief: Naivete unlocks builds expertise prevents; both required.
operational_pattern: Attempt 'impossible' builds; pair with learning from agent output not syntax.
ai_era_implications: Ignorance advantage shrinks as models improve—judgment remains

source_episode: The rise of the professional vibe coder (a new AI-era job) | Lazar Jovanovic (Professional Vibe Coder)
source_quote_or_context: Coding is not the problem we're solving... the problem is clarity.
core_belief: Wrong optimization target is execution velocity not spec quality.
operational_pattern: Spend majority time planning/chatting; treat AI as co-founder educator; read agent narratives.
hidden_assumptions: Code view is source of truth
behavioral_signals: Frustration at 'you know what I mean'
ai_era_implications: Winners optimize clarity and taste; tools commoditize typing

source_episode: The rise of the professional vibe coder (a new AI-era job) | Lazar Jovanovic (Professional Vibe Coder)
source_quote_or_context: If enterprise setup takes two hours, I'll build it myself faster.
core_belief: Builder breakeven vs SaaS moves sharply toward build for custom stacks.
operational_pattern: Default to custom internal tools when integration cost exceeds hours-to-ship with AI.
ai_era_implications: Every function gets a vibe coder; SaaS consolidates to niches

source_episode: The rise of the professional vibe coder (a new AI-era job) | Lazar Jovanovic (Professional Vibe Coder)
source_quote_or_context: AI is an amplifier. If you don't know what you're doing, you produce garbage faster.
core_belief: Tool leverage multiplies prior clarity and taste.
operational_pattern: Invest in domain judgment, security awareness, quality bar before scaling prompts.
hidden_assumptions: Anyone can ship prod day one
pressure_scenarios: Brand damage from bad public builds
contradictions: Quality merch stores vs broken flows
operational_adaptations: Pair vibe coders with security review hooks
ai_era_implications: Garbage faster becomes catastrophic at agent parallelism

source_episode: The rise of the professional vibe coder (a new AI-era job) | Lazar Jovanovic (Professional Vibe Coder)
source_quote_or_context: Coding is going to be like calligraphy... rare art.
core_belief: Manual coding becomes craft signal not default production.
operational_pattern: Don't optimize careers for hand-coding; optimize for product outcomes and orchestration.
contradictions: Weekend C++ by choice on team
ai_era_implications: Roles converge to builder; calligraphy coders niche

source_episode: The rise of the professional vibe coder (a new AI-era job) | Lazar Jovanovic (Professional Vibe Coder)
source_quote_or_context: It's an ideas role... step in when people have ideas but not bandwidth.
core_belief: Centralized builder bandwidth unlocks org creativity.
operational_pattern: Embed rover with high velocity mandate across growth/GTM/community/enterprise.
hidden_assumptions: Ideas belong in product team only
failure_modes: Every team hires own vibe coder
operational_adaptations: Start in growth; expand on proof
ai_era_implications: Head of vibe coding or builder pool becomes standard

source_episode: The rise of the professional vibe coder (a new AI-era job) | Lazar Jovanovic (Professional Vibe Coder)
source_quote_or_context: People like me don't know they're not supposed to be building X... that's how we build it.
core_belief: Expertise can preclude attempts models now make possible.
operational_pattern: Invite naive builders; delay technical naysaying until after attempt.
failure_modes: Desktop apps and Chrome extensions shipped
ai_era_implications: Companies pair naive builders with senior reviewers not gatekeepers

source_episode: A child psychologist's guide to working with difficult adults | Dr. Becky Kennedy
source_quote_or_context: When you look at bad behavior, the actual problem is someone doesn't have the skill they need to manage something happening internally.
core_belief: Misbehavior is skill deficit not identity verdict.
operational_pattern: Diagnose internal skill gaps before character attacks; teach regulation/cooperation skills at work as with kids.
ai_era_implications: AI won't fix adults who lack skills—coaching still core

source_episode: A child psychologist's guide to working with difficult adults | Dr. Becky Kennedy
source_quote_or_context: Separate behavior and identity... quickest way to unproductive conversation is losing sight someone's good inside.
core_belief: Identity safety enables honest performance conversations.
operational_pattern: Frame feedback on behaviors; maintain respect for person's core goodness.
ai_era_implications: Automated surveillance without goodness lens worsens trust

source_episode: A child psychologist's guide to working with difficult adults | Dr. Becky Kennedy
source_quote_or_context: Secure attachment isn't getting it right all the time—it's the presence of repair.
core_belief: Accountable return after rupture builds more trust than faux perfection.
operational_pattern: After harsh meeting: own tone, impact, plan; don't defend; 'perfect is creepy.'
organizational_archetype: Leader who interrupted report in meeting
ai_era_implications: Async AI slights need human repair loops too

source_episode: A child psychologist's guide to working with difficult adults | Dr. Becky Kennedy
source_quote_or_context: Connection forms a bridge so they can walk back to your world to act in shared interest.
core_belief: Correction without connection fails as coercion.
operational_pattern: Join person's reality briefly (taxes-on-couch vs demands); then invite joint problem-solving.
organizational_archetype: Manager needing report to finish urgent work
behavioral_signals: Stakeholder anxiety and identity threat during transition
emotional_political_dynamics: Explicit rituals and scripts to operationalize the pattern
ai_era_implications: Radical candor with real care
Don't use AI to fake connection

source_episode: A child psychologist's guide to working with difficult adults | Dr. Becky Kennedy
source_quote_or_context: Boundaries are what you tell someone else you will do, and it requires the other person to do nothing.
core_belief: Requests are not boundaries; only self-controlled consequences are.
operational_pattern: State what you will do if pattern continues; stop expecting them to change voluntarily.
behavioral_signals: Stakeholder anxiety and identity threat during transition
emotional_political_dynamics: Explicit rituals and scripts to operationalize the pattern
operational_adaptations: Scripts: I will X if Y continues
Train managers on boundary vs request
ai_era_implications: Clear boundaries reduce AI ping-pong escalation loops

source_episode: A child psychologist's guide to working with difficult adults | Dr. Becky Kennedy
source_quote_or_context: Resilience over happiness... people who can say this is hard and I can do hard things.
core_belief: Optimize for capacity to endure difficulty not comfort maximization.
operational_pattern: Celebrate struggle tolerance; avoid fixing all discomfort; build sturdy teams.
organizational_archetype: Exec building culture during hard pivot
emotional_political_dynamics: Fear looking weak naming hard things
ai_era_implications: AI accelerates pace—resilience required to avoid brittleness

source_episode: A child psychologist's guide to working with difficult adults | Dr. Becky Kennedy
source_quote_or_context: Your goal as a leader is to become sturdy.
core_belief: Steadiness under others' dysregulation is the intervention.
operational_pattern: Regulate self first; don't match panic; hold container for hard emotions.
pressure_scenarios: Difficult adult tantrums in exec room
emotional_political_dynamics: Team loses anchor when leader panics
operational_adaptations: Coach leaders on nervous system regulation
Model sturdiness after mistakes via repair
ai_era_implications: Leaders sturdy enough to direct agents without panic spirals

source_episode: A child psychologist's guide to working with difficult adults | Dr. Becky Kennedy
source_quote_or_context: My most generous interpretation... all humans need the same things whether one or forty-five.
core_belief: Assume unmet needs drive ineffective behavior before malice.
operational_pattern: MG I on confusing actions; translate to needs/skills; respond with curiosity.
contradictions: Questions shift from blame to needs
operational_adaptations: Pair with boundaries when pattern persists
ai_era_implications: Don't attribute to AI malice what is context error—parallel for agents

pattern_name: Task Loss Not Job Loss Frame
source_episode: Marc Andreessen: The real AI boom hasn't even started yet
source_quote_or_context: Everybody wants to talk about job loss, but really look at task loss. The job persists longer than individual tasks.
core_belief: Work redesign unit is tasks within jobs not job titles.
operational_pattern: Map task bundles for PM/eng/design; swap tasks via AI; expect titles to lag task reality.
hidden_assumptions: AI replaces companies in one wave
ai_era_implications: Orchestrating agents is new task layer on engineering

source_episode: Marc Andreessen: The real AI boom hasn't even started yet
source_quote_or_context: Mexican standoff between PM, designer, coder—each believes AI lets them do the other two jobs.
core_belief: All three roles are partially right; combination beats silo.
operational_pattern: Encourage cross-skilling with AI; reward triple-threat builders; don't protect stovepipes.
ai_era_implications: Super-empowered individuals outproduce three-person pods

source_episode: Marc Andreessen: The real AI boom hasn't even started yet
source_quote_or_context: Really great people becoming spectacularly great... not twice as good, ten times.
core_belief: AI widens performance distribution at individual level.
operational_pattern: Invest in top talent agency; give leverage tools; expect wider output spread.
operational_adaptations: Homeschool plus AI tutoring for depth
ai_era_implications: Winners are spectacularly great hybrids; hire for agency plus craft

source_episode: Marc Andreessen: The real AI boom hasn't even started yet
source_quote_or_context: If we didn't have AI we'd panic about depopulation... remaining human workers at a premium.
core_belief: Labor scarcity and low productivity growth context reframes AI as necessity.
operational_pattern: Plan for human premium skills; don't assume endless labor supply; pair AI with population trends.
ai_era_implications: Human relationship work gains relative price

source_episode: Marc Andreessen: The real AI boom hasn't even started yet
source_quote_or_context: Productivity growth last 50 years very low... AI hits economy that had almost no technological progress.
core_belief: Felt digital change ≠ measured economic acceleration until now.
operational_pattern: Under-price one-dimensional 'AI eats world'; model interaction with demographics and institutions.
contradictions: Bits progress vs atoms stagnation debate
operational_adaptations: Target sectors with verifiable answers first
ai_era_implications: Reasoning/medical domains with evals see gains first

source_episode: Marc Andreessen: The real AI boom hasn't even started yet
source_quote_or_context: AI transfers sand into thought—the philosopher stone.
core_belief: Fundamental commodity shift is intelligence on tap.
operational_pattern: Teach kids to leverage AI as lever; spend spare hours training with models.
ai_era_implications: Bloom 2-sigma via LLM at scale

source_episode: Marc Andreessen: The real AI boom hasn't even started yet
source_quote_or_context: Huge premium on people able to take responsibility... in order to lead you must learn to obey.
core_belief: Agency matters but structure and discipline enable it.
operational_pattern: Build initiative in kids/ICs; pair with grounding rules; avoid pure rule-following culture.
ai_era_implications: AI rewards agency—prompting is initiative

source_episode: Marc Andreessen: The real AI boom hasn't even started yet
source_quote_or_context: Job of programmer was calculators in rooms... now argue with coding bots.
core_belief: Each abstraction layer redefines programmer tasks not eliminates humans.
operational_pattern: Learn to evaluate agent output; orchestrate parallel bots; depth still matters for debug.
pressure_scenarios: Scripting wasn't real programming fight repeats
operational_adaptations: Teach kids code reading while agents write
ai_era_implications: Historical pattern says humans stay in loop at new abstraction

source_episode: 5 questions to ask when your product stops growing | Jason Cohen (2x unicorn founder)
source_quote_or_context: 100 new customers divided by 5% cancellation = 2,000 customer ceiling.
core_belief: Logo churn creates hard asymptote regardless of marketing hustle.
operational_pattern: Compute max customers from new logos / churn rate; fix churn before scaling acquisition.
operational_adaptations: Prioritize logo churn before channel tests
ai_era_implications: AI can't outmarket exponential churn drag

source_episode: 5 questions to ask when your product stops growing | Jason Cohen (2x unicorn founder)
source_quote_or_context: Changed from 'why did you cancel' to 'what made you cancel'—usable responses doubled.
core_belief: Wording steers churners from excuses to product causality.
operational_pattern: Use open-ended 'what made you cancel'; avoid dropdown noise; randomize any lists.
operational_adaptations: Human reads details AI summarizes themes only
ai_era_implications: LLMs good at themes bad at actionable anomalies—keep human detail pass

pattern_name: Too Expensive Is Never The Reason
source_episode: 5 questions to ask when your product stops growing | Jason Cohen (2x unicorn founder)
source_quote_or_context: They already saw pricing and bought... too expensive is never ever the reason.
core_belief: Stated price objection after purchase signals unmet promise not price sensitivity.
operational_pattern: Five-whys style root-er causes; probe integrations, onboarding, segment fit.
contradictions: Surprise when price raise doesn't hurt
ai_era_implications: AI pricing optimizers miss promise failure

source_episode: 5 questions to ask when your product stops growing | Jason Cohen (2x unicorn founder)
source_quote_or_context: Pricing selects the market... $2/month looks low quality to 400M revenue company.
core_belief: Price is segmentation signal not just demand lever.
operational_pattern: Raise prices to attract serious buyers; expect signups unchanged or up; repeat until elastic.
failure_modes: 12x price test no signup change story
behavioral_signals: Founder emotional attachment to accessible price
operational_adaptations: Iterative price raises with measured signup rate
ai_era_implications: AI features justify premium positioning

source_episode: 5 questions to ask when your product stops growing | Jason Cohen (2x unicorn founder)
source_quote_or_context: Series of questions in order—first problem if you don't fix it, rest doesn't matter.
core_belief: Growth stalls have priority order: logo churn, pricing, NRR, channels, need-to-grow.
operational_pattern: Run five-question diagnostic sequentially; don't tune bottom of funnel if top question fails.
ai_era_implications: Same framework when AI shifts channels—churn still first

source_episode: 5 questions to ask when your product stops growing | Jason Cohen (2x unicorn founder)
source_quote_or_context: If you don't know what to do, focus on onboarding... small front-end shift huge revenue impact.
core_belief: Early lifecycle churn dominates; fixes there multiply.
operational_pattern: Treat onboarding like YouTube first-30-seconds retention; intervene pre-cancel when signals show trouble.
ai_era_implications: AI can personalize onboarding—still highest ROI lever

source_episode: 5 questions to ask when your product stops growing | Jason Cohen (2x unicorn founder)
source_quote_or_context: What good customers have in common that bad customers don't—not what good alone share.
core_belief: Retention work requires discriminative correlates not generic power-user traits.
operational_pattern: Compare successful vs churned cohort behaviors; avoid actions both groups share.
ai_era_implications: AI averages hide discriminative churn signals

pattern_name: Do You Need To Grow Question
source_episode: 5 questions to ask when your product stops growing | Jason Cohen (2x unicorn founder)
source_quote_or_context: Last question: do you need to grow? If you're not growing you're dying—is that true?
core_belief: Not every stall requires growth; investor narrative ≠ company health.
operational_pattern: Ask if growth is necessary now; consider profitable steady state; resist investor slogans blindly.
pressure_scenarios: Founder identity tied to hockey stick
failure_modes: Forced growth into bad fit markets
ai_era_implications: One-person profitable businesses viable in AI era

source_episode: The non-technical PM's guide to building with Cursor | Zevi Arnovitz (Meta)
source_quote_or_context: Created a CTO with custom prompt... I own the problem, you're complete owner of how it's built.
core_belief: Non-technical builders need adversarial technical owner persona before coding agents.
operational_pattern: GPT/Claude project as CTO: challenge ideas, forbid people-pleasing, separate problem from implementation.
organizational_archetype: Non-technical PM at Meta building side products
pressure_scenarios: People-pleaser AI agrees with bad ideas
ai_era_implications: Plan mode in tools replicates part of CTO role

source_episode: The non-technical PM's guide to building with Cursor | Zevi Arnovitz (Meta)
source_quote_or_context: Coding agents eager to write code... planning is really important for payments/database changes.
core_belief: Premature coding agents cause gnarly bugs; enforce planning phase.
operational_pattern: Use ask/plan modes; block build until spec; especially for schema/payment work.
failure_modes: Agent starts coding on vague prompt
ai_era_implications: Native plan modes in tools encode lesson

source_episode: The non-technical PM's guide to building with Cursor | Zevi Arnovitz (Meta)
source_quote_or_context: /review plus Codex and Cursor each review the code.
core_belief: Non-technical builders compensate via redundant model review.
operational_pattern: After agent writes code, orchestrate cross-tool reviews; don't trust single model.
hidden_assumptions: Can't review code so ship blind
contradictions: Engineers ask Zevi to teach them
ai_era_implications: Review bottleneck shifts to orchestration

source_episode: The non-technical PM's guide to building with Cursor | Zevi Arnovitz (Meta)
source_quote_or_context: Start GPT project beautiful UI... graduate Bolt/Lovable... Cursor light... terminal dark mode.
core_belief: Confidence ramp reduces terror of code surfaces.
operational_pattern: Staged exposure: chat-only → no-code builders → IDE light → terminal; learn from agent text not syntax.
ai_era_implications: Every PM can copy ramp; Meta internal demand signal

source_episode: The non-technical PM's guide to building with Cursor | Zevi Arnovitz (Meta)
source_quote_or_context: In coming years everyone's going to become a builder; titles and responsibilities collapse.
core_belief: Function lines blur when PMs ship real products solo.
operational_pattern: Invest in building literacy; engineers teach PM workflows; reward shipped artifacts.
organizational_archetype: Meta PM with engineers asking to learn
operational_adaptations: Success = readers open laptop and build
ai_era_implications: Hybrid PM-builder role formalizes

source_episode: The non-technical PM's guide to building with Cursor | Zevi Arnovitz (Meta)
source_quote_or_context: Best time to be a junior... get out of school and build a startup on your own.
core_belief: Entry-level scarcity offset by unprecedented solo build leverage.
operational_pattern: Juniors should ship side projects with agents; employers value demonstrated builders.
hidden_assumptions: Junior roles gone so give up
ai_era_implications: Agency plus AI beats waiting for headcount

source_episode: The non-technical PM's guide to building with Cursor | Zevi Arnovitz (Meta)
source_quote_or_context: If people walk away thinking how amazing you are, you failed... if they open computer and build, succeeded.
core_belief: Teaching metric is replication not admiration.
operational_pattern: Optimize episodes/docs for copy-paste prompts; engineers should start same day.
ai_era_implications: Content that doesn't enable build is entertainment

source_episode: The non-technical PM's guide to building with Cursor | Zevi Arnovitz (Meta)
source_quote_or_context: Memory feature mixed running with product reviews... projects compartmentalize context.
core_belief: Separate contexts prevent cross-domain contamination in AI work.
operational_pattern: Use projects per domain (PM vs hobbies); avoid global memory for multi-hat roles.
organizational_archetype: PM who is also runner/psychology student
ai_era_implications: Agent memory policies become enterprise requirement

source_episode: How to show up in any room with a low heart rate: Silicon Valley's missing etiquette playbook | Sam Lessin
source_quote_or_context: Etiquette is a skill for how to show up in a room with a low heart rate.
core_belief: Calm presence beats transactional intensity in high-stakes rooms.
operational_pattern: Prepare buffer time; abundance mindset; build relationships not card collection.
organizational_archetype: First-time founder at KP holiday party
ai_era_implications: AI won't replace trust-building at dinners

source_episode: How to show up in any room with a low heart rate: Silicon Valley's missing etiquette playbook | Sam Lessin
source_quote_or_context: When technology is a major deal, etiquette matters for partnerships and data trust.
core_belief: Soft skills compound when tech commoditizes.
operational_pattern: Mirror expected behaviors; hygiene basics; seriousness about enterprise trust.
ai_era_implications: Buyer-side agents still evaluate human trust signals

source_episode: How to show up in any room with a low heart rate: Silicon Valley's missing etiquette playbook | Sam Lessin
source_quote_or_context: Show up with self-confidence and calm of abundance... this isn't your one shot.
core_belief: Relationship building is long game not single conference conversion.
operational_pattern: Give more than take; avoid collecting cards; repeat names with eye contact.
ai_era_implications: Avatars attend meetings—but human trust still gates big deals

source_episode: How to show up in any room with a low heart rate: Silicon Valley's missing etiquette playbook | Sam Lessin
source_quote_or_context: Be early... buffer so heart rate isn't up when you enter.
core_belief: Arrival state shapes entire interaction quality.
operational_pattern: Target a few minutes early; brief wait to compose; simple apology if late—no drama.
ai_era_implications: AI schedules tight—but human first impressions still physical

pattern_name: Don't Make Late People Feel Bad
source_episode: How to show up in any room with a low heart rate: Silicon Valley's missing etiquette playbook | Sam Lessin
source_quote_or_context: If somebody else is late, do not make them feel bad—opposite of your own late behavior.
core_belief: Grace for others' lateness reduces room tension.
operational_pattern: When guest arrives late: welcome simply, move on; no performative punishment.
ai_era_implications: Human rapport still local even when agents schedule

source_episode: How to show up in any room with a low heart rate: Silicon Valley's missing etiquette playbook | Sam Lessin
source_quote_or_context: Things at intersection of hilarious and useful... humor cuts noise but needs deeper truth.
core_belief: Memorable teaching requires comedy plus operational payoff.
operational_pattern: Package etiquette as funny-but-true; double down on viral hooks that teach behaviors.
ai_era_implications: Founders share etiquette memes—distribution via humor

source_episode: How to show up in any room with a low heart rate: Silicon Valley's missing etiquette playbook | Sam Lessin
source_quote_or_context: Ship early ship often... V1 good limited edition, evolves with crowd feedback.
core_belief: Iterative public teaching product like software.
operational_pattern: Launch incomplete playbook; collect gaps; expand categories from users.
ai_era_implications: Same loop for AI product docs and social norms content

source_episode: How to show up in any room with a low heart rate: Silicon Valley's missing etiquette playbook | Sam Lessin
source_quote_or_context: Silicon Valley encouraged abrasive on purpose... etiquette shows trustworthy counterpart.
core_belief: Intentional abrasiveness was over-rotated; trust wins partnerships.
operational_pattern: Keep product edge; soften interpersonal friction; especially with data-sensitive buyers.
operational_adaptations: Pair technical demos with conduct norms
ai_era_implications: As code commoditizes, manners differentiate

source_episode: The power user's guide to Codex: parallelizing workflows, planning techniques, advanced context engineering tips, automating code reviews, and more | Alexander Embiricos
source_quote_or_context: Org set up to try things quickly... aiming fuzzily a year out, empirical on product that lands.
core_belief: When capabilities uncertain, run many fast experiments not annual roadmaps.
operational_pattern: Fuzzy long-range aim; weekly/monthly empirical bets; humble learning loops.
organizational_archetype: AI product org post-Dropbox startup founder
ai_era_implications: Few companies can run true bottoms-up at AI speed

source_episode: The power user's guide to Codex: parallelizing workflows, planning techniques, advanced context engineering tips, automating code reviews, and more | Alexander Embiricos
source_quote_or_context: Codex is beginning of software engineering teammate... smart intern that won't read Slack unless asked.
core_belief: Agent must expand across SDLC not just autocomplete.
operational_pattern: Pair today; push toward proactive teammate across plan/validate/maintain; inject Datadog/Slack context.
ai_era_implications: Winning agents = coding agents with computer use

source_episode: The power user's guide to Codex: parallelizing workflows, planning techniques, advanced context engineering tips, automating code reviews, and more | Alexander Embiricos
source_quote_or_context: Limiting factor is human typing speed or human multitasking speed.
core_belief: AGI adoption gated by human orchestration bandwidth before model IQ.
operational_pattern: Design for parallel agents; reduce review friction; async workflows.
ai_era_implications: Next breakthrough is human-agent bandwidth interfaces

source_episode: The power user's guide to Codex: parallelizing workflows, planning techniques, advanced context engineering tips, automating code reviews, and more | Alexander Embiricos
source_quote_or_context: Major goals: make easier to review code, not just write code.
core_belief: Post-agent era bottleneck is human verification throughput.
operational_pattern: Invest in review acceleration; proactive teammate reduces raw PR count confusion.
ai_era_implications: Products that don't solve review fail at agent scale

pattern_name: Any Agent Should Be Coding Agent
source_episode: The power user's guide to Codex: parallelizing workflows, planning techniques, advanced context engineering tips, automating code reviews, and more | Alexander Embiricos
source_quote_or_context: If you want to build any agent, maybe you should be building a coding agent.
core_belief: Computer use via code is universal interface for action.
operational_pattern: Route agent capabilities through code generation and execution; leverage terminal/composer patterns.
operational_adaptations: Code as lingua franca for agents
ai_era_implications: Non-coding agents hit walls; coding agents eat scaffolding

source_episode: The power user's guide to Codex: parallelizing workflows, planning techniques, advanced context engineering tips, automating code reviews, and more | Alexander Embiricos
source_quote_or_context: Build tool so it feels maximally accelerating rather than unclear what human should do.
core_belief: UX success is clarity of human role when agent acts.
operational_pattern: Monitor Reddit complaints; tune product to reduce ambiguity about next human action.
ai_era_implications: Agents that obscure human role get abandoned

source_episode: The power user's guide to Codex: parallelizing workflows, planning techniques, advanced context engineering tips, automating code reviews, and more | Alexander Embiricos
source_quote_or_context: Sora Android fully new app 18 days, public 28 days total.
core_belief: Extreme compression of ship timeline with Codex leverage.
operational_pattern: Study internal workflows enabling 18-day greenfield; replicate patterns not just tools.
ai_era_implications: Timeline expectations reset industry-wide

source_episode: The power user's guide to Codex: parallelizing workflows, planning techniques, advanced context engineering tips, automating code reviews, and more | Alexander Embiricos
source_quote_or_context: Large part of goal is proactive teammate helpful by default—not tens of prompts a day but thousands of benefits.
core_belief: Shift from invoked AI to ambient helpfulness requires proactive agents.
operational_pattern: Design Codex to initiate useful work with permissions; expand beyond prompt-driven usage.
ai_era_implications: Delivering AGI benefits requires proactive coding agents with guardrails
